title: Close final-component rename identity race
goal: Determine and, where the Linux kernel permits it, close the remaining race between descriptor-relative rename and post-effect inode capture so a same-privilege actor cannot replace the final source or destination name during the effect boundary without a deterministic fail-closed recovery result.

acceptance.assertion: A syscall-interposed or otherwise deterministic test replaces the final source or destination name specifically after renameat returns and before Bureau captures the moved inode; the task distinguishes transient interference, false success and incomplete recovery without inferring exploitability from theory alone.

The implementation records which Linux primitives can and cannot provide conditional name-to-inode rename semantics, including renameat2, openat2, file handles, leases or an equivalent coordination mechanism; unsupported claims are rejected rather than approximated.

If an enforceable primitive exists, apply and rollback use it symmetrically with no silent fallback; otherwise Bureau exposes an explicit residual-risk result and requires a stronger exclusive coordination precondition before mutation.
Tests prove that Bureau never reports success for the wrong inode and never compensates or deletes an object whose captured device/inode does not match the object moved by the current run.
The stronger boundary preserves idempotent receipts, multi-entry compensation and bounded performance, or documents a justified schema transition and operational migration path.
All adversarial validation uses temporary synthetic roots; no active Bureau state migration, rollback or deletion is performed.

metadata.observation: T013 binds ancestors and top-level entries by device/inode, performs descriptor-relative effects and compensates detected post-effect identity drift. Linux renameat remains name-based and does not atomically assert the expected source inode while renaming.

risk_classification.impact_if_confirmed: A same-privilege actor racing after renameat and before effect capture could force a fail-closed but incompletely recoverable result; T013 prevents false success but does not claim atomic rename-by-inode.
risk_classification.current_mitigation: No-follow anchored descriptors, per-effect path and inode checks, post-effect identity verification, captured-effect compensation and typed incomplete-recovery errors.

lease_scope_migration.rationale: The residual rename race is located in the state-root artifact effect implementation.
does_not_establish: safe mutation of terminal historical tasks
